## [00:00 - 03:01]

It's 1991. I'm 20 years old and I'm working in a McDonald's in Milford, Massachusetts. I'm flipping burgers and my boss Pam comes, she takes me by the arm and she pulls me into the walk-in cooler and she says she has to ask me a question. Now Pam is a 40-something mother of three who desperately wants to be 20 again. She's the woman who buys us beer but brings the kegs to the party in hopes that we will allow her to stay at the party and party with us. So Pam looks at me and she says, Lisa is getting married, Lisa's a woman we work with and I want to have a bachelorette party here in McDonald's for her. And I nod because I don't actually know what a bachelorette party is so it sounds fine and I don't know what she's about to ask me. So she puts her hand on my shoulder and she says, we want you to be the stripper for the bachelorette party. Now I say yes for two reasons. First, I'm 20 so I can only see five minutes in front of me so I don't know really what this means. But I'm also the guy who doesn't get girls because of the way I look. I have no game. The only move I have is proximity. I find a girl I like and I stand next to them for as long as possible and I try to wear them down. I am like romance's version of erosion and it's worked like I've had girlfriends. But for the first time in my life someone wants to see like what's under my clothes and yes she's older than my mother and yes she's my boss but it's a start and so I'm excited. But I ask Pam I say I don't want to be totally naked and she says we do not want you to be totally naked either. We'll get you a thong and I say great and so the night of the bachelorette party comes and I show up in my uniform because that's what she's told me to wear and as I'm going into the store I'm still excited because again I can only see five minutes in front of me and I'm like excited about having this opportunity and I feel that way until I walk into the store and there's all guys working that night because the girls are in the back getting ready and as I walk in the guys look at me and they have a look that I can't quite figure out but it is universal and it is disconcerting and I start to think that maybe I have made a mistake. I meet Pam in the back hallway where we stack the buns, the Big Mac buns and the hamburger buns and she hands me an orange thong and she tells me to put it on and then knock on the crew room door when I'm ready. Now I don't know if you know what a thong for a man looks like but it's essentially a plastic sandwich bag with two strings attached and as I'm putting it on I am not attempting to be grandiose about my situation in any way whatsoever but Pam has not like sized the thong. It is like the Happy Meal version of
